verdict.text: Florianópolis scores Moderate, making it one of the safer Brazilian cities for tourists. The island capital of Santa Catarina is a popular domestic and Argentine tourist destination, and its affluent demographics translate to lower crime rates. All three advisories cover it under Brazil's general framework but without specific escalation.

reconciliation.text: Petty theft is the dominant risk — pickpocketing at beaches (Jurerê Internacional, Ingleses, Lagoa da Conceição) and in the Centro. Car break-ins at beach car parks are common. Some northern mainland areas of Greater Florianópolis (São José) carry higher risk.

The island itself is very manageable for visitors. Lagoa da Conceição, Centro Histórico, and the northern beaches are tourist-friendly. Use normal urban precautions (valuables in hotel safes, apps for night transport). This is one of the most relaxed risk profiles on this list.

Official statistics: World Bank / UNODC — national intentional homicide rate for Brazil: 2023: 19.3/100k; 2022: 20.1/100k; 2021: 20.5/100k; 2020: 21.8/100k. Note: this is a country-level rate, not city-specific. Source: World Bank Open Data (VC.IHR.PSRC.P5), compiled from UNODC Crime Trends Survey.
